message:
refactor(util/time): factor RFC3339 + relative-time parsing into typed helpers

Substantially expand `parse_flexible_timestamp` and the surrounding
parsers in `src/util/time.rs` into named helper functions with typed
errors, so future edits to each parsing class don't have to wade
through the master `if let Ok(x) = ...; if let Ok(y) = ...` chain.

src/util/time.rs:
- Introduce private `RelativeTimeError { InvalidUnit, OutOfRange }`
  so relative-time parsing ("3h", "2d", "1w") can distinguish a
  typo from a value that overflows `Duration::hours`/`days`/`weeks`.
  Previously both collapsed into a generic parse-error with no
  way for callers to tell them apart.
- Add `parse_rfc3339_timestamp(s) -> Option<DateTime<Utc>>` that
  wraps `DateTime::parse_from_rfc3339` + `with_timezone(Utc)`,
  returning None on failure so the master parser can fall through
  to the next format.
- Extract `parse_relative_time(s) -> Result<Duration, RelativeTimeError>`
  to centralize the "N<unit>" parsing. Uses `IntErrorKind`
  discrimination on parse failures so an out-of-range value
  (e.g. `"99999999999999d"`) returns OutOfRange instead of the
  generic InvalidUnit.
- Rewrite `parse_flexible_timestamp` to call the new helpers in
  sequence; behaviour equivalent but the master function is now
  one flat dispatch table.

src/storage/schema.rs:
- `row_bool` helper reformatted onto multi-line form per rustfmt's
  width rule (was 100+ cols). Same logic.
- `test_v7_rebuilds_content_hashes_and_marks_dirty` setup now
  splits the long `conn.execute("INSERT INTO export_hashes ...")`
  call onto multiple lines — pure rustfmt.

tests/conformance.rs, tests/storage_id_hash_parity.rs:
- Two `assert_eq!` calls collapse back to single-line form after
  rustfmt re-evaluated their width. Purely cosmetic.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
